_schema_note: 1.1 = test_results split into backend/frontend sub-objects (established W10); everything else mirrors the W9 1.0 shape.
wave: Wave A + A.2 (live-run correctness batch + god console)

resolution.summary: QE gate for the 12-item Wave A + A.2 batch on build/wave-a-live-run-fixes: EM-129 (humanized building names + raw_name payload), EM-132 (build_step-on-damaged redirects to repair), EM-133 (contribute_funds clamped at the remaining gap), EM-134 (per-building animal-damage cooldown, 6 ticks, first hit always lands), EM-108 (governance location gate at resolution time, kind-based not id-based), EM-135 (reroute-aware lane health: first-attempt budget bump after 2 truncations in a 6-outcome window), EM-130 (keyword-mapped building styles + neutral fallback, humanized kind subtitles), EM-131 (deterministic render-side slot layout, no Math.random in placement), EM-106 (named backend_data volume for data/), EM-136 (god_intervene bless_energy/grant_credits with 1..100 validation + before/after payload), EM-137 (one-shot god whisper riding exactly the target's next context), EM-138 (GOD CONSOLE panel: WORLD EVENTS / INTERVENE / VOICE groups, living-agents-only selector, contract-exact request shapes). All 12 adversarial verdicts came back 'confirmed' — zero refuted, zero CRITICAL, zero MAJOR; 20 MINOR observations recorded (literal-text and coverage-gap class, none a behavioral defect). Full gates re-run by the QE agent: backend 330/330, web 182/182, vite build green.

contract_conformance.notes: Verified against contracts/wave-a.md + contracts/wave-a2-god-console.md via 12 independent adversarial audits, every one 'confirmed' with criterion-by-criterion evidence (REPL probes, diff-vs-parent checks, grep for relocated violations, schema-drift checks both directions). Highlights: EM-129 humanizer hits every named edge (snake->Title, junk->fallback to agent-name form, kind stays raw for EM-130, additive raw_name); EM-132 truly reuses action_repair (no duplicated logic) and all four other invalid statuses keep their pre-commit failure messages; EM-133's clamp makes funds overshoot impossible at the single funds_committed write site and self-heals legacy overshot snapshots via the zero-gap soft-fail; EM-134's cooldown is animal-only (arson unaffected), sentinel-defaulted so the first hit lands, and excluded from to_dict() as mandated; EM-108 gates on place kind=='governance' (proved with a non-townhall governance place), emits the verbatim guidance string, exempts no-governance worlds, and god paths bypass it by construction; EM-135's first-attempt formula is identical to the retry boost, repaired truncations still report truncated=True, clear_cache() flushes lane state on world reset, zero new chat() call sites; EM-130 covers every contract keyword with substring-trap ordering (archive beats arch, garden beats den) and the subtitle always shows the humanized kind, never a style name; EM-131's slotLayout is pure sorted-id math with mesh-level (not just point-level) clearance; EM-106's volume traces end-to-end to the actual in-container DB path (/app/data/run.sqlite) and `docker compose config --quiet` exits 0; EM-136/137 follow the billboard guarded-seam pattern exactly (503/422 mapping, same emit+broadcast helpers, 280 cap byte-identical to BillboardBody, one-shot pop with only one production call site); EM-138's request shapes match the backend pydantic models field-for-field including omitted-amount defaults. Docked one point for small but real literal deviations: (1) EM-129 truncates raw_name at 60 chars where the contract says the raw arg is preserved; (2) EM-134's contract-quoted 'shooed away' feed text never renders (pre-existing in-character None-handlers used instead — runtime.py is Agent R's file, so Agent W could not add it); (3) EM-136's endpoints return 200 where the 'mirror billboard exactly' clause implies 201 (the contract is internally inconsistent; the explicit '200 happy paths' test clause was followed, and the frontend only checks res.ok); (4) EM-138's VOICE group contract names a proclamation control that has never existed in web/src — unsatisfiable as written, nothing real was omitted. All four are MINOR, none changes behavior against intent.

test_coverage.notes: Backend +55 tests in three NEW files, all adversarially audited as non-tautological (exact-string asserts, post-mutation state asserts, negative invariants like 'rejected god requests emit nothing'): backend/tests/test_wave_a_world.py 25 (EM-129 humanizer edges + raw_name + fallback, EM-132 redirect + all-other-statuses-keep-failing incl. offline's distinct message, EM-133 overshoot/exact-fit/zero-gap/partial-then-overshoot/insufficient-credits, EM-134 first-hit/6-tick-block/expiry/arson-bypass/to_dict-absence, EM-108 fail-away+succeed-at for both actions, kind-not-id, no-governance exemption); backend/tests/test_lane_health.py 13 (boost engage/disengage, healthy-lane passthrough, guarded getattr for duck-typed routers, E2E next-turn bumped budget asserted at 2048); backend/tests/test_god_console.py 17 (clamp at 100, exact payload dicts incl. before/after, inclusive 1/100 boundaries, 0/101/-5/'lots' rejections, dead-agent rejection both kinds, whisper one-shot + other-agents-never-see with adversarial assembly ordering, persisted rows, 503 matrix). Frontend +32 in new files: worldSpace.test.ts 15 (keyword table incl. live offenders prepare_beds/village_fair/community, substring traps, neutral fallback distinct from monument, 12-id multi-ring layout with pairwise distance > 3.5, anchor clearance, shuffle stability), ControlPanel.god.test.tsx 10 (groups via role=heading, living-only selector excluding a dead fixture, in-flight disable via held promise, 422 error path as role=alert), api.god.test.ts 7 (real fetch URL/method/body asserts incl. amount omission, 422/503/500/network mapping). Honest gaps (all recorded by the adversarial audits): (a) the _validate_world passthrough letting damaged buildings reach the EM-132 redirect (agents/runtime.py:696-700) has NO direct test — flagged independently by both the EM-132 and EM-135 audits; a validator-layer regression re-blocking damaged would go uncaught; (b) EM-106 has no automated compose-validation step (manual `docker compose config --quiet` only; contract names no test file for Agent I); (c) EM-131's label declutter is satisfied by reusing the EM-102 proximity gating, not newly asserted; (d) mock-mode behavior of the new INTERVENE buttons (they bypass the parent-prop mocking seam) is untested.

security.notes: New surface this wave = POST /api/god/intervene and POST /api/god/whisper, reviewed with the adversarial audits plus the contract's threat framing. Input validation: intervene's pydantic body pins kind to the two known values at the engine seam (unknown kind -> ValueError -> 422), agent_id resolved server-side via _living_agent_or_raise (unknown/dead -> 422, resurrection impossible), amount Field(ge=1, le=100) at the API plus an independent 1..100 re-check at the engine seam, so the clamp/validation holds even for direct seam callers; whisper text Field(min_length=1, max_length=280) byte-identical to the existing BillboardBody, re-capped by BILLBOARD_TEXT_CAP slice at the seam. Prompt injection: the whisper IS the designed injection channel — it is rendered inside a clearly-framed 'A voice only you can hear' block in _assemble_context, capped at 280, consumed exactly once (single production pop site, grep-verified), and never enters any other agent's context (adversarially tested with assembly-order games); no other model-reachable text path was added. No new LLM calls anywhere in the wave (free-scale law audited per item). No auth regression: both endpoints sit on the same unauthenticated local single-user god surface as /api/billboard, /api/proclaim, /api/runs/fork and the reset/spawn/kill controls — posture unchanged and accepted by design (this is the same accepted finding as the W11b report). 503 guarded-seam pattern prevents uninitialized-world 500s; ValueErrors map to 422, never stack traces. Residual notes: bless/grant are state-mutating and unauthenticated (in line with the existing surface, listed for the record); the engine seam's int(amount) accepts Python bools (unreachable through the API, pydantic rejects bool); whispers queued for an agent that dies before its next turn linger in pending_whispers (bounded by manual POSTs, not a growth vector).

regression_risk.notes: Full backend suite 330/330 and full web suite 182/182 with the new tests in place; vite build green (pre-existing >500kB chunk warning only). The adversarial audits specifically hunted for relocated/silenced violations: the only edits to pre-existing test files across the whole wave are EM-108 fixture relocations to the townhall (git numstat shows additions ONLY, zero deletions — and the fact that 5 pre-existing files needed relocation is itself evidence the new gate fires in real flows); no assertion was deleted or weakened anywhere; test_json_mode.py's duck-typed routers pass untouched under the guarded-getattr wiring. Event/snapshot contracts stayed additive (raw_name, amount_requested/amount_applied, redirected_from, god_intervention/whisper_posted are all new optional surface; events.schema.json payloads are open objects; last_animal_damage_tick and pending_whispers deliberately excluded from contract surfaces). Known live-behavior shifts, all in-contract but worth watching in gate 3: (1) EM-133 changed the meaning of the pre-existing economy `amount` payload key in the clamped case from requested to applied — the only frontend consumer (economy throughput selector) wants applied, but event-log docs should note the semantic shift; (2) EM-131's ring re-packing can visibly teleport an existing building when a sibling is added mid-run (pure function of the changed id list, contract-permitted); (3) EM-134's cooldown resets on snapshot reload because the field is contract-mandated out of to_dict(), so the first post-restore animal hit always lands; (4) the commemorative-rule fuzzy match now runs on the display name — verified behavior-preserving because _commemorated_rule normalizes case/punctuation identically (W11b commemorative tests stay green); (5) god_intervention/whisper_posted events vanish from category-focused feed views (no CATEGORY mapping), matching the pre-existing proclamation_issued house style.

frontend.command: cd web && npm run test (vitest run)
frontend.changed_existing: none — new coverage lives in new sibling files (worldSpace.test.ts, ControlPanel.god.test.tsx, api.god.test.ts); EM-138's commit is add-only

test_results.frontend_verification: cd web && npm run test — 182/182 across 26 files; npm run build — clean (pre-existing >500kB chunk warning only)

issues.title: EM-132 validator passthrough (damaged -> build_step allowed) has no direct test — flagged by two independent audits
issues.detail: backend/petridish/agents/runtime.py:696-700 now lets status='damaged' pass _validate_world so the world-side repair redirect is reachable in live turns, but no test exercises this layer (EM-132's tests call World.action_build_step directly). The behavior was manually verified correct by the auditor (damaged+co-located -> None; operational/destroyed/abandoned/offline keep their messages; co-location gate intact), so this is a coverage gap, not a defect. Recommend one validator-layer test in a follow-up.

issues.title: EM-129 raw_name is truncated at 60 chars, not the fully untouched raw arg
issues.detail: backend/petridish/engine/world.py:1028 stores str(name)[:60] as raw_name; the contract says 'the raw arg is preserved in the returned event payload'. Matches the pre-existing Building.name cap so impact is negligible, but it is strictly a contract-text deviation. Related cosmetic Title Case quirks ('2nd_market' -> '2Nd Market', non-ASCII lowercase names skip Title Case, possible trailing space at the 60 cap) violate no named criterion.

issues.title: EM-133 changed the semantics of the pre-existing economy `amount` payload key in the clamped case
issues.detail: backend/petridish/engine/world.py:1142 — `amount` now carries the applied (clamped) value instead of the requested value. Within the additive-only rule (no rename/removal/type change), keeps amount == -credits_delta consistent, and the only frontend consumer (economy throughput, web/src/inspector/selectors.ts:516) wants applied — but the event-log docs should record the semantic shift. amount_requested/amount_applied carry both values explicitly.

issues.title: EM-136/138 minor inconsistencies inside an internally inconsistent contract
issues.detail: (1) /api/god/intervene and /api/god/whisper return 200 while the billboard endpoint they 'mirror exactly' returns 201 — the contract's binding tests section explicitly says '200 happy paths', the implementation follows it, and the client only checks res.ok; a future strict 201-pinning client would diverge. (2) The VOICE group contract names 'billboard reply + proclamation controls' but no proclamation control exists anywhere in web/src (only the backend POST /api/proclaim) — the billboard reply was moved correctly; the commit message overstates what shipped. (3) In mock mode the new BLESS/GRANT/WHISPER buttons call the real API client directly (unlike inject/billboard which route through mockable parent props) and fail gracefully with the inline 'backend unreachable' message — a behavior asymmetry, not a break.

issues.title: EM-134 contract feed text 'shooed away' never renders; cooldown resets across snapshot restore
issues.detail: (1) The cooldown outcome renders the pre-existing in-character None-handlers ('paws at X but nothing happens' / 'bumps X, leaving it unmoved') in backend/petridish/animals/runtime.py:623,633 — runtime.py is Agent R's file, so Agent W could not add the literal 'shooed' phrasing; the substantive requirement (harmless, in-character, no silenced turn) is met. (2) Because last_animal_damage_tick is contract-mandated OUT of to_dict(), the first animal hit after a snapshot restore always lands even if a hit occurred <6 ticks pre-snapshot — a direct consequence of the contract's own rule, noted for awareness.

issues.title: EM-130 buildingStyle exact lookup is prototype-unsafe for keyword-shaped kinds
issues.detail: web/src/components/world3d/worldSpace.ts:159 — BUILDING_STYLES[kind] on a model-authored kind like 'constructor' or 'toString' returns a truthy Object.prototype member instead of a style. Pre-existing pattern (the old code had the same hole) and out of contract scope; an Object.hasOwn guard or null-prototype map would close it.

issues.title: Remaining MINOR observations (summarized; none behavioral defects)
issues.detail: EM-131: slotLayout re-packs rings as the per-place count grows, so an existing building can visibly jump slots when a sibling is added (contract-permitted: pure function of the changed id list); the pre-existing Math.random() bob-phase in Structure.tsx seeds cosmetic y-sway only, not position. EM-130: humanizeKind preserves ALL-CAPS rest-of-word ('VILLAGE_FAIR' -> 'VILLAGE FAIR'), consistent with the backend EM-129 humanizer. EM-135: commit 9fef06e bundles the out-of-item EM-132 validator passthrough (within Agent R's owned file, necessary for the redirect to be live-reachable; covered by the dedicated issue above). EM-136: engine-seam int(amount) accepts Python bools (API-unreachable; pydantic rejects bool); god_intervention/whisper_posted have no feed CATEGORY mapping so they vanish from category-focused views (matches proclamation_issued house style; feed chips are out of this wave's scope). EM-137: whispers for an agent that dies pre-delivery linger in pending_whispers (bounded by manual POSTs; a pop-on-death would be tidier); a whisper is consumed even if every LLM attempt that turn fails (in-spec: delivery is defined as riding the next context). EM-106: no automated compose-config check in CI (manual `docker compose config --quiet` passed; contract names no test file for Agent I).

gate_decision.reason: All 12 adversarial verdicts (EM-129, EM-132, EM-133, EM-134, EM-108, EM-135, EM-130, EM-131, EM-106, EM-136, EM-137, EM-138) returned 'confirmed' — zero refuted, zero CRITICAL, zero MAJOR — and every gate re-run by QE is green: backend 330/330 (55 new tests across test_wave_a_world.py / test_lane_health.py / test_god_console.py, all audited as non-tautological), web 182/182 (32 new tests for worldSpace mapping+slots and the GOD CONSOLE), npm run build clean. Contract conformance verified criterion-by-criterion against wave-a.md and wave-a2-god-console.md with only MINOR literal-text deviations (raw_name 60-char cap, 'shooed' phrasing, 200-vs-201 inside an internally inconsistent contract clause, a contract-named proclamation control that never existed); the new god endpoints validate input at both API and engine seams, confine the designed whisper injection to a framed one-shot 280-char block, add zero LLM calls, and introduce no auth regression for the accepted local single-user posture. No assertion anywhere was weakened (add-only diffs outside contract-sanctioned EM-108 fixture relocations). The 7 recorded issues are MINOR coverage gaps and notes for the event-log docs/gate 3, none blocking. GREEN to proceed to the orchestrator gate.
